title: hieracium taurinense subsp. neoprenanthes
intro: Hieracium taurinense subsp. neoprenanthes, also known as mountain hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant. The plant's health benefits include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve skin conditions, and support immune function. Hieracium taurinense subsp. neoprenanthes can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
